task_completion.description: Log in to NOSH ChartingSystem as the administrator.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The NOSH ChartingSystem dashboard is visible, indicating a successful login (e.g., user profile or admin settings are accessible).

task_completion.description: Navigate to the patient form templates section.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The interface shows a section for managing, creating, or editing form templates within the NOSH system.

task_completion.description: Create a new form template titled 'COVID-19 Screening'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A form builder or form settings page is visible with the title or name field explicitly set to 'COVID-19 Screening'.

task_completion.description: Add a 'Has Fever?' field with a Yes/No type.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A field labeled exactly 'Has Fever?' is visible in the form builder, and its input type is set to Yes/No, Checkbox, Radio, or Boolean.

task_completion.description: Add a 'New Cough?' field with a Yes/No type.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A field labeled exactly 'New Cough?' is visible in the form builder, and its input type is set to Yes/No, Checkbox, Radio, or Boolean.

task_completion.description: Add a 'Travel History' field with a Text type.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A field labeled exactly 'Travel History' is visible in the form builder, and its input type is set to Text, Text Area, String, or Input.

task_completion.description: Save the form and ensure it is set as an active template.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The form is shown as saved in a list of templates, and there is an indication that it is 'active' (e.g., a checked 'active' box, a status toggle, or listed under active forms).

integrity.description: The agent used the web GUI to create the form.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser interacting with the NOSH UI. There should be no evidence of the agent using terminal commands (like curl or direct database SQL inserts) to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual NOSH ChartingSystem.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots display the genuine NOSH ChartingSystem interface (menus, headers, styling) rather than a local text editor or a mock HTML page.

integrity.description: The form was created using the proper form builder interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: The fields and title are entered into the correct form builder interface elements, not just typed into a generic search bar or a single unstructured text document.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target form title must be exactly 'COVID-19 Screening'. The required fields must be labeled exactly 'Has Fever?', 'New Cough?', and 'Travel History' (do not accept abbreviated labels like 'Fever' or 'Cough'). The first two fields must be Yes/No (or checkbox/radio/boolean) types, and the third must be a Text (or text area/string/input) type.
